title: 'If she refuses ... ’: Bengaluru woman's husband records chilling video, then stabs her 20 times
content: A Home Guard was brutally murdered by her husband in Bengaluru after he lured her to a reconciliation meeting. The accused, addicted to online betting, stabbed Manjula multiple times in front of their children. He had reportedly expressed his intention to kill both himself and his wife in pre-recorded videos. The husband has been arrested.

title: RSS must register, pay taxes: Karnataka minister Priyank Kharge writes to Mohan Bhagwat
content: Karnataka Home Minister Priyank Kharge has urged RSS chief Mohan Bhagwat to disclose the organization's legal status, finances, and tax compliance. Citing the RSS's extensive presence and public mobilization, Kharge argued for accountability in a constitutional democracy. He called for transparency, suggesting formal registration and adherence to Indian laws, especially following recent remarks on university VCs attending RSS events.

title: Explained: Why India A were penalised 10 runs against Sri Lanka A
content: In a bizarre turn of events during a cricket match, Sri Lanka A was awarded 10 runs before their innings began. India A was penalized twice for repeated pitch infringements by their batsmen, leading to the unusual scoreline. Despite the setback, India A posted 265, thanks to crucial half-centuries.

title: Economist Mark Zandi, who warned of US recession, agrees with ban on Anthropic Fable AI models
content: Moody's Analytics chief economist Mark Zandi warns that sudden U.S. government restrictions on Anthropic's advanced AI models pose a significant economic threat. This move, coinciding with the historic SpaceX IPO, highlights market vulnerability to regulatory shocks and investor optimism over AI's potential.

title: Watch: Kheer spills on Kerala minister during launch of free bus rides for women
content: During the launch of Kerala's free bus travel scheme for women, Minister Bindu Krishna had payasam spill on her inside a crowded KSRTC bus. The "Priyadarshini" scheme, aimed at women's empowerment, allows free travel on ordinary buses, with the government to reimburse the transport corporation.

title: India's T-90 tanks may get active shield against missiles, drones and top-attack weapons
content: India's DRDO is developing an advanced Active Protection System for T-90 tanks, capable of intercepting threats at over 1,500 m/s, including top-attack munitions. This indigenous technology aims to significantly boost tank survivability against modern threats like ATGMs and drones, marking a major step in defence self-reliance.

title: Almost a year after giving engineers Claude and Cursor, Disney says, 'minimise AI-coded products'
content: Disney is urging its engineers to use AI tools like Claude and Cursor efficiently, focusing on boosting development speed without compromising code quality. The company aims to prevent AI-generated products from failing post-release, emphasizing productivity over excessive AI token usage. This strategic push follows a failed billion-dollar partnership with OpenAI.

title: Looking for a rental home in Bengaluru? Check demand, rents and emerging hotspots
content: Bengaluru’s rental landscape is on the rise, witnessing a remarkable uptick of nearly 10% in the Jan-Mar quarter and an impressive annual growth of 16.4%. Driven by the influx of professionals and students, popular neighbourhoods such as Whitefield and Sarjapur Road are experiencing high demand.

title: Snicko in football? Cricket-style technology steals the show during FIFA World Cup clash
content: A novel ball-contact technology, akin to cricket's 'Snicko', made its debut in a FIFA World Cup 2026 qualifier between Sweden and Tunisia. This innovation proved crucial in overturning an offside call, awarding a goal to Sweden's Mattias Svanberg. The technology confirmed a touch by Alexander Isak, leading to a 5-1 victory for Sweden.

title: US senator says Tesla benefited from govt support, Elon Musk replies
content: Elon Musk has refuted claims that his companies, Tesla and SpaceX, owe their success to government aid, stating such incentives represent less than 2% of their value. The debate ignited between Senators Mike Lee and Ed Markey over taxpayer contributions to Musk's wealth. Musk asserted that removing EV tax credits actually boosted Tesla's market share.

title: Explained: India's groundwater crisis and why Hyderabad tops the list of most affected cities
content: India's groundwater crisis is deepening. Hyderabad leads metropolitan areas in depletion. Karnataka faces widespread water shortages. Marathwada's tanker deployment has surged. Punjab battles extraction and uranium contamination. These regions highlight growing pressure on water resources. Urgent recharge, sustainable management, and conservation are vital to avert further worsening.

title: From 0–2 down to 3–2 up: India fight back to beat USA in FIH Nations Cup
content: Deepika scored twice for India, while Navneet Kaur added the third goal. Ashley Sessa and Madeleine Zimmer scored for the USA. The winner of the Nations Cup will earn promotion to the FIH Pro League, from which India were relegated last season.

title: Jewar Airport is now operational. Is there still room for property prices to rise?
content: Noida's Jewar International Airport's launch is set to ignite real estate growth along the Yamuna Expressway. Experts foresee a surge in demand for homes, offices, and commercial spaces, potentially boosting capital values by 15-20% annually. The airport's economic ecosystem is expected to create jobs, attracting both residents and investors to this emerging NCR hub.

title: Pakistan Air Force training aircraft crashes near Mardan, both pilots killed
content: A Pakistan Air Force training aircraft crashed near Mardan, killing both pilots during a routine mission. This incident marks the latest in a series of military aviation accidents in Pakistan, with authorities investigating the cause. Condolences have been extended by top military and government officials to the bereaved families.

title: 'First step, not end of story': Sitharaman hints at more measures to attract foreign capital inflows
content: FM Sitharaman described the recent initiatives taken by the Reserve Bank of India and the government to attract overseas investment as an initial move, signalling that additional measures could follow to encourage greater foreign capital inflows.

title: Rupee rallies to five-week high on crude oil plunge; RBI measures add to momentum
content: The Indian rupee strengthened against the US dollar for a second consecutive day, buoyed by a significant drop in crude oil prices following a preliminary US-Iran agreement. This rise also builds on recent Reserve Bank of India measures aimed at attracting dollar inflows. Traders anticipate further support for the rupee, though its appreciation pace may hinge on the RBI's policy.

title: Heavy rain lashes parts of Delhi-NCR; IMD issues red alert, warns of winds up to 90kmph
content: Delhi experienced a sudden weather shift on Monday afternoon with a dust storm and strong winds, prompting the IMD to issue a red alert for thunderstorms and rainfall. The capital saw wind speeds of up to 92 kmph, with further rainfall and gusty winds expected. This change is attributed to a western disturbance affecting the Himalayan region.

title: UK PM Starmer announces ban on social media apps for children under 16
content: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer announced a landmark ban on children under 16 from major social media platforms, aiming to protect them from harmful content and online dangers. This "Australia-plus" model targets platforms like TikTok and Instagram, with stricter rules for gaming and AI chatbots. The government emphasizes protecting children's safety and happiness, with enforcement expected by spring 2027.

title: Kanpur man ends life after power outage kills aquarium fish
content: A 22-year-old man in Kanpur allegedly died by suicide after his pet fish perished due to a prolonged power outage. His family reported he was deeply attached to the aquarium inhabitants. The incident occurred in the Cantonment area, with police investigating the death, which appears to be a suicide. The body has been sent for post-mortem.

title: Writer Mayukh Ghosh on how Karisma Kapoor shed vanity in ‘Brown’
content: Writer Mayukh Ghosh shares insights into the making of Karisma Kapoor's series 'Brown'. He details crafting the complex character of Rita Brown, an alcoholic cop. Ghosh also highlights his creative partnership with director Abhinay Deo. Their collaboration focused on authenticity and capturing Kolkata's diverse essence. The series explores grief and resilience.

title: Eric Trump dragged into UFC fight rigging row after viral message claim
content: Eric Trump has strongly denied claims that he privately contacted UFC commentator Daniel Cormier before UFC Freedom 250. The controversy began when a screenshot appeared online showing an alleged conversation between Trump and Cormier. The messages suggested that Trump had asked whether any fighters were injured and whether any bouts on the White House card would be fixed.

title: Norway crown prince's stepson sentenced to four years in prison over rape, domestic violence
content: Marius Borg Hoiby, the son of Norway's Crown Prince, has been sentenced to four years in prison after being found guilty of two counts of rape and domestic violence. The trial unveiled distressing aspects of the case, stirring public discourse and casting a shadow over the royal family's reputation.

title: Islam Makhachev mocks Ilia Topuria after shock loss, fans fire back
content: Islam Makhachev did not waste time reacting after Ilia Topuria suffered the first defeat of his professional MMA career at UFC Freedom 250. The historic event ended with Justin Gaethje becoming the new UFC lightweight champion after a dominant performance against the previously unbeaten star. Topuria entered the fight as the favorite and was expected to defend his title successfully.

title: Gurgaon couple alleges baby mix-up at Delhi fertility clinic; FIR filed
content: A Gurugram couple alleges a child swap at a Greater Kailash IVF center after a DNA test failed to confirm maternity and paternity of one of their newborns. Doubts arose when one baby didn't resemble the family, prompting the DNA test.

title: NEET-UG 2026 refund scam foiled; Bihar student arrested for hacking candidates' accounts
content: NEET UG 2026 cyber fraud: Gujarat Police's Cyber Crime Branch, in coordination with the NTA, has arrested a 19-year-old Bihar student for allegedly attempting to divert NEET UG 2026 fee refunds into his own bank accounts. Investigators found that the accused targeted around 350 candidate accounts and gained unauthorized access to nearly 150 accounts with weak passwords. The fraud was detected through the NEET portal's security systems, enabling authorities to trace and apprehend the accused before widespread financial losses occurred.

title: Flat delayed by over 15 years; consumer court orders refund, Rs 5 lakh compensation
content: A Delhi consumer court has ordered a Ghaziabad housing society to refund Rs 79 lakh to two homebuyers for a 15-year delay in flat possession. The society, deemed deficient in service, must also pay Rs 5 lakh in compensation. Despite arguments about its cooperative status and RERA registration, the court upheld its responsibility to consumers, citing a Supreme Court ruling.

title: Kumkum Bhagya actress Sanchita Ugale found hanging at home near Mumbai
content: TV actress Sanchita Ugale, known for her role in 'Kumkum Bhagya', has reportedly died by suicide at the age of 22. She was found hanging in her Nalasopara residence and was declared dead at a hospital. Police have registered an Accidental Death Report and are investigating the circumstances.

title: 'I'd be lying if I said I wasn't depressed': Shreyanka after India's win over Pakistan
content: India began their Women's T20 World Cup campaign with a 64-run win over Pakistan, powered by Deepti Sharma's five-wicket haul and Smriti Mandhana's 68. After the victory, Shreyanka Patil revealed she battled depression and even considered quitting cricket during her injury layoff. The all-rounder credited her family's support and love for the game for helping her return.

title: NCERT textbook shows Indus Valley's 'Dancing Girl' with covered torso to make it 'age-appropriate'
content: NCERT's new Class 9 arts textbook has altered the appearance of the Indus Valley Civilisation's 'Dancing Girl' artefact. The traditionally bare torso is now visually covered in the new edition. This change has drawn criticism from experts who argue it misrepresents the original 4,500-year-old bronze sculpture. The matter is currently under review by NCERT.

title: ITR filing FY 2025-26: Can you switch between new and old income tax regime every year?
content: ITR filing: The question that needs to be answered is: if your tax regime choice can change every year, depending on your financial situation and stage of life, can you switch between regimes every year as well?

title: Kyiv cathedral hit by US-patriot missile? Russia denies strikes on Ukrainian Unesco site
content: Russia denies striking the historic Kyiv Pechersk Lavra monastery. Moscow claims a US-made Patriot missile hit the UNESCO site. Ukraine insists a Russian attack caused significant damage. The incident occurred during a large-scale Russian assault across Ukraine. President Zelenskiy condemned the strike as an attack on religion and heritage. The dispute highlights differing narratives of the ongoing conflict.

title: Germany script history, overtake Brazil for major World Cup record
content: Germany's FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign kicked off with a historic 7-1 thrashing of debutants Curaçao, propelling them past Brazil as the all-time leading World Cup scorers with 239 goals. Despite an early equaliser from Curaçao, Germany dominated, with Kai Havertz scoring twice. This resounding victory boosts Germany's confidence ahead of their next match.

title: Sshura Khan gives a befitting reply to a troll who called Arbaaz Khan 'old man'
content: Makeup artist Sshura Khan humorously shut down a troll questioning her marriage to Arbaaz Khan by calling him a "limited-edition vintage model." She also revealed their first date was due to a lost bet and shared details about their daughter's name and motherhood. The couple, who married in December 2023, met on the sets of 'Patna Shukla'.

title: '20 people at party, all drunk': Mystery shrouds Hyderabad techie's death during team farmhouse outing
content: A software engineer's life was tragically cut short at a farmhouse party in Yadadri-Bhuvanagiri. Gandla Ramu was discovered lifeless in a swimming pool following an alleged altercation during a team outing. While police have registered a murder case based on the father's complaint, the exact cause of death awaits the post-mortem report.

title: Watch: Controversial hand gesture during Germany vs Curacao goes viral
content: Shaun Evans, a referee involved in the FIFA World Cup 2026, is under scrutiny after allegedly making an upside-down 'OK' gesture during Germany's match against Curacao. Critics claim the sign is linked to white supremacist groups, prompting Fare Network to demand his removal. FIFA has yet to comment, while the gesture has sparked controversy in sport before.

title: 89% students abroad eye Indian jobs as work-abroad dream loses its shine
content: Indian students pursuing overseas education are increasingly reassessing where their futures lie. Data from Student Circus shows an 89% rise in engagement with India-based job opportunities between 2023 and 2024, a trend that has remained strong since. As immigration pathways tighten abroad and India's corporate ecosystem expands, globally educated students are viewing the country not as a fallback option, but as a destination offering growth, stability, and long-term career potential.

title: Eight dead, 7 injured as van carrying pilgrims fell into well in Maharashtra
content: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis announces compensation of ₹5 lakh to the families of the deceased.

title: Khan Sir got my brother murdered: Rival tutor Raushan Anand after bail from court in vandalism case
content: Mr. Khan, who could not be traced since an FIR was lodged against him upon the statement of one of his injured bodyguards that they had opened fire under pressure from their influential boss, came out with a video message debunking the allegations

title: Chandrababu Naidu meets Singapore PM Lawrence Wong, seeks partnership in semiconductors and AI
content: The Chief Minister met India’s High Commissioner and venture capitalists, and pitched Andhra Pradesh’s strengths in electronics and natural farming

title: Unscientific pruning, neglect threaten Thiruvananthapuram’s tree wealth
content: Several tree-lover groups in the city are lobbying ardently for the protection of old trees, primarily on social media, stressing their collective value to society

title: Delhi court takes cognisance of ED chargesheet, refuses bail to former RCom executive
content: The case stems from allegations that large sums raised through bank loans were diverted and routed through a nework of overseas subsidiaries and offshore entities.

title: International Yoga Day 2026: Main event to be held in Kolkata on June 21; PM to lead
content: Union Minister for Ayush said 2026's theme, ‘Yoga for Healthy Ageing’, highlights the role of yoga in promoting physical health, mental well-being and healthy ageing

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: Rain in parts of Delhi; Monsoon remains low in A.P. due to El Niño conditions
content: The monsoon activity in Andhra Pradesh has remained subdued thus far, with 17 out of the 28 districts reporting deficient rainfall between June 1 and June 14

title: Congress, BRS, MIM parties enabling ‘illegal infiltrators’ in voter lists: BJP Telangana chief
content: If BJP benefited in West Bengal because of SIR, how did UDF win in Kerala, questions BJP Telangana president Ramchander Rao

title: ‘Priyadarshini’ free bus travel scheme gets rousing start in Kerala’s Thrissur
content: Home Minister Ramesh Chennithala flags off district-level launch; he calls it a ‘golden moment’ in Kerala’s history

title: Teams deployed in Kukatpally, Serilingampally and Quthbullapur to address monsoon issues
content: Phone numbers of the field officers concerned zone-wise were shared for faster coordination and prompt response during emergencies

title: Head constable dies in road accident during operation against illegal sand mining in Ramanathapuram
content: Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay condoled the death of the head constable and announced a solatium of ₹30 lakh to the bereaved family, a release stated

title: We are the largest parliamentary force from West Bengal: NCPI in a social media post
content: 20 rebel MPs of Trinamool Congress have announced their decision to merge with NCPI. The MPs met  the Speaker of Lok Sabha Om Birla in Delhi on Sunday and submitted a letter to that effect

title: Kannur Crime Branch nabs notorious vehicle thief after 22 years
content: The accused, identified as Baldev Singh (60), a native of Ghodi in Palwal district of Haryana, is wanted in several vehicle theft cases across northern Indian States and is also an accused in around 10 vehicle theft cases registered at Kannur Town police station in 2004

title: India, Slovakia decide to elevate ties to status of Comprehensive Partnership, says PM Modi
content: Robert Fico said Slovakia and India are linked by several common interests and expressed admiration for India’s achievements across multiple fields

title: Attacks on vessels with Indian seafarers, Iranian ‘service fee’ may feature in IORA meet
content: India is the current Chair of the Indian Ocean Rim Association; Although the IORA’s charter explicitly bars members from raising issues that do not concern regional cooperation, in the broader scheme of things, India could look to utilise the forum for airing its concerns in the maritime space

title: ‘Insects can kill a serpent’: Pawan Kalyan warns of divisive forces at ‘national integrity’ meet in Delhi
content: The Deputy Chief Minister said it was the party’s responsibility to pass on patriotic values to Gen Z and teach them their rights and duties

title: Udhayanidhi demands high-level meeting of police to discuss law and order in T.N.
content: Mr. Udhayanidhi was reacting to the death of a three-year-old child after she was sexually assaulted near Gummidipoondi

title: Climate change, social isolation heighten risks for elderly: HelpAge India study
content: 78% of respondents had experienced at least one climate-related hazard in the last three years, with heatwave (45%), flood (27%) and drought (20%) being the most commonly reported events

title: Supreme Court stays High Court proceedings on challenges to new transgender law
content: A Bench headed by the Chief Justice indicated that it may either transfer all the petitions to the top court or consolidate them and assign them to a single High Court

title: 20 rebel Trinamool Lok Sabha MPs seek to merge with Nationalist Citizen Party of India
content: Meet Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla and announce that they are merging with the registered regional party for time being

title: Lingayat associations oppose Kaneri mutt seer visit to Vijayapura for Hindutva convention
content: Warn of protest citing charge of insulting fellow seers, misusing Basavanna’s name to propagate Hindutva

title: NCPI: The Howrah-based political outfit that may house 20 rebel TMC MPs
content: Founded in 2015 by Shuili Kundu, the NCPI aims to position itself as a viable alternative amidst TMC’s electoral struggles

title: Assam preparing to welcome ‘powerful global leader in July’, says Assam CM
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is expected to hold a bilateral summit with his Japanese counterpart, Sanae Takaichi, in Guwahati

title: Kerala launches ‘Priyadarshini’ free bus travel scheme for women, transgenders
content: Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan inaugurates UDF govt’s flagship scheme at Thampanoor. Inaugural journey carrying CM and other dignitaries to Secretariat operated by women crew

title: Odisha distributes voter roll revision forms to 99% electors; 34.5% digitised
content: Odisha CM Mohan Charan Majhi participated in the SIR campaign at his native Raikala village in Keonjhar district, filling out his enumeration form

title: Retrenchment of 15,000 contract workers sparks unrest in Odisha, BMS seeks CM’s intervention
content: Bharatiya Mazdoor Sangh alleges retrenchments were carried out by four power distribution companies operating under Tata Power in Odisha in the name of cost-cutting.

title: CMRL pay-off case: Company MD Sasidharan Kartha’s daughter appears before ED for questioning
content: ED likely to ask Shiby S. Kartha about services offered by Veena’s now-defunct firm Exalogic Solutions to CMRL

title: Telangana schools reopen; breakfast scheme launched for pre-primary to 12 standard students at govt institutions
content: Millet idlis, puri, dosa, upma and bonda to be served with milk or ragi jawa to be served to students

title: 9.8 lakh deletions in Odisha’s voter rolls after electoral mapping process
content: Around two lakh Form-7 applications received after April 2, 2026 have been kept on hold; letter from the Office of the Chief Electoral Officer directs Electoral Registration Officers to carry out door-to-door survey, and physically verify the authenticity of voter rolls, which impacts final deletion

title: NCERT shades image of Indus Valley ‘dancing girl’ in new class 9 art textbook
content: The original bronze statuette, discovered at Mohenjo Daro, is depicted nude except for multiple bangles on one arm and a necklace; in the new textbook, it appears in a darker shade, with parts of the torso covered compared to its original form

title: Shuhaib murder: Kerala HC allows father to be impleaded in case
content: Court allows State’s request to link another pending petition filed by accused challenging the mode of examination conducted by trial court. In this plea, HC had set aside Thalassery court’s order to cancel bail granted to accused

title: Police open fire to thwart robbery attempt on Chennai-Vijayawada train in Bapatla
content: A manhunt is under way, with 10 special teams formed to trace the gang and one suspect detained for questioning

title: Author-entrepreneur Indira Lankesh passes away in Bengaluru
content: While her husband, noted Kannada writer and journalist P. Lankesh, resigned as a professor and took to making films and producing plays, Indira Lankesh supported him by selling sarees from her house

title: Kerala free travel scheme hits row as some commuters allege KSRTC rebranded Ordinary buses as City Fast services overnight
content: A section of commuters and critics have alleged that there have been concerted attempts by the public utility to rebrand ordinary buses as City Fast buses to bypass the scheme’s norms

title: Deaths in Brazil raise concerns about India’s dengue vaccine, DengiAll
content: Brazilian authorities have stressed that the 42 cases of severe adverse events out of half-a-million vaccinated represent only 0.008%. There is no doubt that it is a small risk at the population level. At the individual level, however, even one life lost to a severe adverse event is one too many

title: Revival of Kidderpore Dock has reduced turnaround time at Kolkata Port says Century Ports director
content: Century Ports Limited, is a 100% subsidiary of Century Ply and has entered into a 30-year contract with Syama Prasad Mookerjee Port, Kolkata, under a public private partnership (PPP) model for operationalization of Kidderpore Dock complex

title: TMC calls rebels' NCPI merger 'ridiculous'; BJP says exodus reflects party's ideological vacuum
content: The dissident camp claimed that 20 former MPs of Trinamool Congress had backed the move and would support the NDA in Parliament

title: Police rescue two teenagers from drowning at Vadarevu and Suryalanka beaches in Bapatla
content: Marine and local police pulled two 17-year-olds from deep waters at Vadarevu and Suryalanka, the Superintendent of Police said

title: Enrolment in third phase clinical trials for India’s first dengue vaccine nearly complete, says ICMR
content: There is currently no antiviral treatment or licensed vaccine for dengue in India, which ranks among the top 30 countries with the highest incidence of the disease

title: PM Modi welcomes West Asia deal; hopes peace will be restored
content: The Prime Minister said India looks forward to deliberations on the remaining issues reaching a sustainable final agreement

title: Firing in Manipur: Three youths injured in Kangpokpi, brought to Imphal for treatment
content: The injured, all belonging to the Kuki community, were brought to the Regional Institute of Medical Sciences (RIMS) in Imphal for treatment by security forces

title: Former zilla panchayat member urges sufficient supply of fertiliser for farmers in Yadgir district
content: Former Zilla Panchayat member Devraj Naik demanded that GESCOM ensure sufficient power supply to farm pump sets for at least seven hours in a day

title: Activists welcome Supreme Court’s victim protection plan for trafficking survivors
content: The court-mandated plan covers every stage, from rescue to reintegration, for survivors of sex trafficking. Activists call for State-level action to implement the Supreme Court’s new victim protection framework

title: Census 2027: Primary, high school teachers form sizeable section of Census enumerators in Kerala
content: Supervisors drawn from higher secondary and high school teachers. Lower division and upper division clerks too included as enumerators and supervisors, say Census officials

title: Congress slams PM Modi's ‘blind devotion to Israel’, says national interest demands greater balance
content: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh said the news that the U.S. and Iran will be signing an agreement on June 19 in Geneva to halt hostilities in West Asia is to be welcomed, even though the full details are yet to be made public officially

title: AMMA row: Ansiba Hassan approaches court seeking FIR against actor Lakshmipriya, woman cop
content: Ansiba, a former joint secretary of AMMA, in her complaint accused association vice-president Lakshmipriya of filing a false complaint against her, and Reshma, sub-inspector of Thripunithura Women’s cell, of subjecting her to harassment and unlawful detention in the name of verifying the allegations against her

title: Disclose tender process for Board exam answer sheet procurement under RTI Act: CIC to CBSE
content: After setting aside the CBSE’s earlier denial of information under the RTI, the transparency panel asked it to furnish revised replies; the Commission found that the Central Public Information Officer had denied information under multiple exemption clauses ‘without giving any proper justification’

title: Anantapur to host conference on drought, natural farming on June 17
content: AF Ecology Centre Director Y.V. Malla Reddy says the June 17 conference will bring together farmers, scientists, and officials to discuss climate-resilient agriculture in drought-hit Anantapur

title: Centre allocates ₹8,500 crore for Bengal under VB-G RAM G scheme, announces CM Suvendu Adhikari
content: CM Suvendu Adhikari  said 1,100 'Jan Kalyan Shivir' camps, being organised from June 15 to June 17, will help people access 54 Central and State government schemes

title: Sabarimala gold case: Kerala Devaswom Minister seeks withdrawal of TDB circular appointing officials facing probe
content: K. Muraleedharan says the Devaswom department can decide on further action in the gold loss case only after SIT files its chargesheets in the two cases

title: The Curious Case Of Sudip Bandopadhyay, Newest Face Of Trinamool Rebels
content: Sudip Bandopadhyay is known to be a soft-spoken leader who always maintained a balanced relationship with different sides.

title: Hormuz Strait, Nuclear Program, Lebanon: Key Terms Of US-Iran Peace Deal
content: Iran and the US have both said the Strait of Hormuz would start to reopen and the US blockade on Iranian ports start to lift as soon as the memorandum is signed.

title: Russia Unleashes Major Attack On Ukraine, 11 Dead, Historic Site Damaged
content: The attacks on the capital, Kyiv, and the second-largest city, Kharkiv, came after Zelensky and Russian President Vladimir Putin spoke separately by phone with US President Donald Trump on Sunday.

title: Homes Crack, Road Splits In Two As J&K Village Sinks
content: Villagers allege that the subsidence is a direct consequence of National Highway construction in the area.

title: "Come Back Or I'll Kill You": Bengaluru Man Stabs Home Guard Wife To Death
content: Pradeep arrived at the house on Sunday carrying a knife hidden inside his jacket and pleaded with Manjula to forgive him and return to him.

title: "Scattered Opinion": Top Court Halts High Courts' Transgender Law Proceedings
content: The Centre sought the clubbing of petitions pending before various high courts challenging the constitutional validity of the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Act, 2026.

title: G7 Summit 2026: How India Is Emerging As Key Global And Strategic Power
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will participate in the upcoming G7 Summit to be held in Evian, France, on June 16-17, 2026.

title: Fresh Border Standoff As Bangladesh Guards Refuse To Take In Own Citizens
content: Tension had gripped the India-Bangladesh border in Assam's Mankachar after nine individuals were found stranded in the no-man's land.

title: BJP, AAP Turn To Pilgrimage Schemes For Voter Outreach In Punjab
content: Traditional issues such as agriculture, unemployment and law and order remain central to Punjab's politics, but parties increasingly recognise that cultural symbolism and welfare-linked pilgrimages can help shape voter perceptions

title: Not Maharashtra Or Gujarat: This State Borrows Most Number Of Small Loans
content: Fewer borrowers are falling behind on repayments.The improvement has been visible across lender categories, including banks, SFBs, and NBFCs.

title: Prioritise AI Ecosystems, Not Just AI Models: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella
content: The Microsoft CEO argued that the priority should shift from building frontier AI models to building broader AI ecosystems, while emphasising that human capital will become even more valuable in the AI era.

title: Video: Dust Storm, Winds At 92 Kmph Speed Hit Delhi, Red Alert Sounded
content: Light to moderate rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms, lightning and strong winds of 60-70 kmph, gusting up to 80 kmph, is expected in several parts of the city.

title: US, India Join Hands To Counter China's Supply Chain Dominance
content: The US-India Business Council has launched an industry platform aimed at building resilient critical minerals supply chains between the two countries, as Washington and New Delhi accelerate efforts to reduce dependence on China-dominated resource networks.

title: Explained: Bengal Teacher Recruitment Scam Investigated By Probe Agency ED
content: The agency is questioning the Trinamool's Diamond Harbour MP about alleged money laundering, financial transactions, and irregularities in the recruitment process associated with the case.

title: He Wasn't Ready To Welcome Trinamool Rebels Into His Party. Then A Twist
content: The NCPI shot to national limelight yesterday after a rebel MP declared they would merge with the little-known political outfit from Bengal.

title: Fight Over Dripping Laundry In Hyderabad Locality, Cops Called In
content: During the fight, some members of one family allegedly assaulted individuals from the other house, prompting residents to alert the police.

title: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: Trump Says Peace Deal To Be Signed On Friday, Tehran Reiterates "Mistrust"
content: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei reportedly denied that a memorandum of understanding would be signed.

title: Stanford researchers find a 'bomb-like' immune cell hidden inside flatworms
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. title: 'If Iran had a nuclear weapon...': Trump calls Bibi 'difficult guy', says he should thank him

title: Palau-flagged ship Settebello had ignored close to 60 warnings before it was hit: US official
content: the Palau-flagged vessel at the centre of the standoff had ignored close to 60 verbal warnings before it came under fire from US forces last week . it also did not heed repeated display of force by military aircraft like flares and flyovers meant to warn against violating the blockade .

title: Unsubscribed: 200 Stanford grads opt out of Sundar Pichai's commencement speech
content: hundreds to 200 students marched out, chanting "Free, free Palestine" the protests targeted the silicon Valley giant's contraceptive contraceptives . a protest was organized by students for justice in Palestine and no Tech for Apartheid .
